query: /* select#1 */ select `testdb`.`orders`.`id` AS `id`,`testdb`.`orders`.`user_id` AS `user_id`,`testdb`.`orders`.`total` AS `total`,sum(`testdb`.`orders`.`total`) OVER (PARTITION BY `testdb`.`orders`.`user_id` ORDER BY `testdb`.`orders`.`created_at` )  AS `running_total` from `testdb`.`orders` where (`testdb`.`orders`.`user_id` <= 100)

inputs.operation: Index range scan on orders using idx_user over (user_id <= 100), with index condition: (orders.user_id <= 100)

functions: sum(orders.total) OVER (PARTITION BY orders.user_id ORDER BY orders.created_at )
operation: Window aggregate with buffering: sum(orders.total) OVER (PARTITION BY orders.user_id ORDER BY orders.created_at )
